place: Barclay CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Barclay Farmstead, first settled by John Kay in the early 1680s, remains one of only three families to own the property for nearly 300 years.
Whippany’s name means "place of the willows," a nod to the willow trees that once lined the banks of its river.
The Whippany River powered the town’s early mills, shaping its industrial heritage and still flowing through the heart of the community.
Today, Barclay Farmstead serves as a living history museum, welcoming visitors to step back into colonial life during public tours.
Local residents protect the river’s health through the Whippany River Watershed Action Committee, ensuring clean water for future generations.
The river cuts through Whippany before joining the Passaic near Pine Brook, linking the town to New Jersey’s larger watershed.
